props.headline: Content blocks for public website systems.
props.body: Use resource, article, changelog, and author blocks to model blogs, guides, announcements, and editorial pages.

props.title: Latest resources
props.body: Blog cards help teams review content hubs and editorial navigation before final copy is ready.

posts.title: Reviewing a landing page wireframe
posts.excerpt: Check first-viewport promise, proof, conversion path, and FAQ coverage.

posts.title: Pricing page questions
posts.excerpt: Use plans, comparison rows, social proof, and contact handoff to remove friction.

posts.title: About page content model
posts.excerpt: Structure values, team, careers, rich text, and resources as reusable sections.

props.title: A public prototype is a content decision tool
props.dek: The goal is not to simulate production. The goal is to make the website story visible enough to critique.
paragraphs: Public websites depend on hierarchy, proof, and trust. Draftpine blocks make those parts explicit as static sections.
Because the output is plain HTML, every route can be reviewed locally, captured in screenshots, and revised without framework setup.

props.name: Draftpine editorial
props.bio: Notes and examples for building compact public website prototypes with static blocks.

props.title: Changelog
props.body: Use changelog blocks for release notes, product updates, or public roadmap summaries.
